events.title: Eastern Market — Weekend Market & Flea
events.description: Historic public market plus weekend outdoor flea, arts, and crafts. Live music when weather permits.

events.title: Capitol Hill Farmers Markets
events.description: Seasonal weekend farmers markets with local produce and vendors.

events.title: Lincoln Park
events.description: Large Capitol Hill green space with playground, paths, and statues. Dog-friendly. Cafes at both ends.

events.title: Garfield Park
events.description: Neighborhood park with playground, tennis, basketball, bocce, and a community garden.

events.title: Yards Park & Navy Yard Waterfront
events.description: Riverfront park with fountains, boardwalk, and frequent seasonal weekend programming.

events.title: National Mall & Smithsonian Museums
events.description: Free Smithsonian museums and monuments. Reliable all-weather, all-ages option.

events.title: United States Botanic Garden
events.description: Free conservatory and gardens at the foot of the Capitol, with a Children's Garden.

events.title: Anacostia Riverwalk Trail Ride
events.description: Paved riverfront trail for a family bike ride, with Capital Bikeshare access nearby.

events.title: The Fields at RFK — Community Open Play & Disc Golf
events.time: Open hours vary (8 AM–dusk disc golf)
events.venue: The Fields at RFK Campus (401 Oklahoma Ave NE)
events.description: Turf fields with community open-play hours plus DC's free disc golf course. 10-min walk from Stadium-Armory.

events.title: CHAW Gallery (Capitol Hill Arts Workshop)
events.venue: Capitol Hill Arts Workshop (545 7th St SE)
events.description: Rotating gallery shows of local artists, two blocks from Eastern Market. Check site for current exhibition.

events.title: How Shakespeare Saved My Life
events.description: A tour-de-force one-man performance by playwright and Broadway performer Jacob Ming-Trent, in which he dares to rescue himself from the 'slings and arrows' of his past. Runs through July 5, 2026 — the first weekend only.

events.title: Miró and the United States — Phillips Collection
events.description: The Phillips Collection's exhibit explores the artistic conversation between Spanish artist Joan Miró (1893–1983) and American artists from the 1940s to the 1960s, tracing Miró's relationships with American artists, collectors, and institutions. On view through July 5 — the July 4 weekend is your last chance to see it.

events.title: Steve Byrne at DC Improv
events.description: Steve Byrne is a crowd favorite bringing positive energy and high-octane stand-up to DC Improv's main room for the July 4th weekend.

events.title: Great American State Fair (DC250)
events.description: A 16-day exposition presented by Freedom 250 anchoring the National Mall through July 10. More than 150 exhibits showcase state industries, crafts, and culinary traditions. Daily themed programming includes Military & Veterans Appreciation Day, Innovation & Technology Day, and Future of America Day, plus patriotic concerts and an Independence Day celebration on July 4. Spans the July 4 weekend fully.

events.title: Great American State Fair (Salute to America 250) – National Mall
events.venue: National Mall (14th St to 4th St NW)
events.description: The centerpiece of America's Semiquincentennial celebration, the Great American State Fair transforms the National Mall into 50 miniature state fairs June 25–July 10. All 50 states plus U.S. territories have pavilions with local heritage, interactive exhibits, live craft workshops, culinary showcases of regional specialties, a Ferris wheel, and daily cultural programming. Free admission; optional paid tastings.

events.title: Andrew Orolfo at Arlington Cinema & Drafthouse
events.description: Filipino-American comedian Andrew Orolfo, a JFL New Face 2022 and Comedy Central/Netflix alumnus, headlines two nights at Arlington Drafthouse with two shows each night.

events.title: Damon Wayans Jr. at DC Comedy Loft
events.description: Damon Wayans Jr. brings his stand-up to DC Comedy Loft for a multi-night run covering the July 11 weekend.

events.title: Broccoli City Festival
events.venue: Washington, DC (venue TBC — check official site)
events.description: DC's beloved annual Broccoli City music and culture festival returns July 11–12, 2026. The festival celebrates hip-hop, R&B, and Black culture with major headliners and community programming. Specific venue and lineup TBD — check the official site for updates.

events.title: Feeling Afraid As If Something Terrible Is Going to Happen
events.description: A darkly comedic one-man show that tackles everything from the fear of intimacy to the terror of vulnerability. Plays through July 12, 2026.

events.title: Marcus D. Wiley at DC Improv
events.description: American stand-up comedian, speaker, and charismatic performer Marcus D. Wiley headlines DC Improv for a multi-night run spanning the July 11 weekend.

events.title: Farmers Market SW at The Wharf
events.venue: M Street Landing, The Wharf (outside Pitango, Philippe Chow & Fish Shop)
events.description: The Farmers Market SW operates every Saturday morning at M Street Landing at The Wharf, running through November. Shop local produce, specialty foods, and artisan goods along the Potomac waterfront.

events.title: Beetlejuice – The Musical (Broadway Tour)
events.description: The hit Broadway musical comedy 'Beetlejuice' comes to the National Theatre for a two-week run July 7–19, 2026.

events.title: Chris D'Elia at DC Improv
events.time: 7:30 PM (Fri Jul 17 confirmed); additional shows Sat–Sun
events.description: Chris D'Elia, known for his podcast 'Congratulations with Chris D'Elia,' performs a stand-up run at DC Improv over the July 18 weekend.

events.title: Eastern Market Weekend Outdoor Market (Saturdays & Sundays)
events.description: Eastern Market's beloved outdoor weekend market runs every Saturday and Sunday. Saturdays feature a vast assortment of local produce, flowers, plants, packaged food, and handcrafted goods. Sundays (Sunday Market, 10 am–4 pm, June–September) feature local craftsmen, artisans, clothing, and apparel. A Capitol Hill community institution for over 150 years.

events.title: FIFA World Cup 2026 Fan Zone — National Mall
events.time: Opens 1 hour before first match; closes 1 hour after last match
events.venue: National Mall, between 3rd and 4th Streets NW
events.description: An official FIFA World Cup Fan Zone on the National Mall runs the entire tournament (June 11–July 19), covering all three target weekends. Features massive screens showing live World Cup matches — all USA games and all knockout-round fixtures — plus interactive exhibits, cultural showcases, youth programming, food, and music. Set between 3rd and 4th Street against the backdrop of DC's monuments. Free and open to the public; register for tickets online. On July 4, open until midnight.

events.title: Lewis Belt at DC Improv
events.description: Oakland-born stand-up comedian, actor, and producer Lewis Belt headlines DC Improv for a weekend run.

events.title: Lotus and Lily Festival
events.description: See blooming lotus and lily flowers, do morning yoga, and unwind with forest bathing at the Lotus and Lily Festival, held July 18–19 in the Anacostia area. A free, nature-focused community event.

events.title: National Cannabis Festival
events.venue: RFK Festival Grounds, 2400 East Capitol St NE
events.description: The National Cannabis Festival returns to the RFK Festival Grounds July 18–19, featuring a weekend of live music, advocacy panels, and cannabis culture programming. Tickets start at $65+.

events.title: No Exit (Scena Theatre at Atlas)
events.description: Scena Theatre presents Jean-Paul Sartre's existentialist classic 'No Exit' at the Atlas Performing Arts Center, running June 25 – July 19, 2026.

events.title: The Wharf World Cup Watch Zone (Summer-long)
events.time: Match-dependent; check wharfdc.com for daily schedule
events.description: The Wharf hosts a dedicated World Cup watch zone on a big screen throughout the tournament (June 11–July 19), covering all three target weekends. Matches shown daily on the waterfront. Check the Wharf's event calendar for specific daily match times — multiple games shown each day including knockout rounds.

events.title: WABA Adult Learn to Ride Classes (Ongoing Summer Sessions)
events.venue: Various DC locations (see waba.org/dc-classes for specific dates/sites)
events.description: WABA's Adult Learn to Ride class teaches complete beginners to balance, glide, pedal, and steer in about three hours. Bikes and helmets are provided. Supported by DDOT and offered at multiple DC locations throughout the summer. Check waba.org/dc-classes for any sessions scheduled on the target weekends.

events.title: WABA Youth & Teen Learn to Ride Classes (Ongoing Summer Sessions)
events.venue: Various DC locations including Anacostia Boat Ramp Lot and others (see waba.org/dc-classes)
events.description: WABA's Youth Learn to Ride class teaches kids ages 6–12 beginner biking skills through games and drills with a 3:1 instructor-to-student ratio. A separate teen class is also offered. Students must bring their own properly sized bike and helmet. Check waba.org/dc-classes for sessions on target weekends.

events.title: Macbeth – STC Academy Summer Reps
events.time: Various (Jul 16, 18, 19, 22, 24, 25 dates shared with Twelfth Night in rep)
events.price: $25 general public; $40 to see both Macbeth and Twelfth Night
events.description: The STC Academy Class of 2026 performs Macbeth in repertory with Twelfth Night as the culmination of their MFA training. Shakespeare's propulsive tragedy asks what happens when ambition overtakes conscience.

events.title: Twelfth Night – STC Academy Summer Reps
events.price: $25 general public; $40 to see both Macbeth and Twelfth Night
events.description: The STC Academy Class of 2026 performs Twelfth Night in repertory with Macbeth as the culmination of their MFA training. Directed by Jess Chayes. Specific performance dates: Jul 16, 18, 19, 22, 24, and 25.

events.title: Beetlejuice The Musical (Broadway Tour)
events.description: The first official touring production of the Broadway smash hit Beetlejuice comes to DC's National Theatre July 7–26, covering all three target weekends. Based on Tim Burton's beloved film, the show follows Lydia Deetz and a demon with a thing for stripes in a visually spectacular, darkly comic musical about family and love. Recommended for ages 13 and up.

events.title: Pippin
events.description: Stephen Schwartz's (Godspell, Wicked) musical masterpiece of purpose, ambition, lust and glory, thrillingly staged by Matthew Gardiner at Signature Theatre in Arlington, VA. Runs through July 26, 2026.

events.title: Sheryl Underwood at DC Improv
events.time: 7:00 PM / 9:30 PM (Fri Jul 24 confirmed); additional shows Sat–Sun
events.description: Emmy-award-winning co-host of 'The Talk' on CBS, Sheryl Underwood brings her fearless stand-up—tackling race, politics, sex, and life—to DC Improv for the July 25 weekend.

events.title: What Became of Us
events.description: A second production running concurrently at Signature Theatre in Arlington through July 26, 2026. Described as a beautiful and moving play about two siblings striving for connection over the course of a lifetime.

events.title: We the Woolly: Remixing 250
events.price: $15–$93; $20 for 30-and-under; Pay-What-You-Can performances at start of run
events.description: Woolly Mammoth's July 2026 production running the full month. Woolly is known for bold, new work. PWYC performances and $20 under-30 tickets available.

events.title: Max Meisel — Cellar Loft Debut at DC Comedy Loft
events.description: LA-based comedian Max Meisel makes his Cellar Loft debut. He is currently featuring for Tom Arnold on a sixty-city tour and is the creator of the BackDoor Comedy show.

events.title: WIT: Escape — Washington Improv Theater
events.time: 9:00 PM (Fri Jul 31); 4:00 PM / 6:00 PM / 8:00 PM (Sat Aug 1)
events.description: WIT's 'Escape' performance series brings longform improv comedy to multiple shows over the August 1 weekend. All shows are audience-driven and unscripted.

events.title: Andy Haynes at DC Improv
events.description: New York City-based stand-up and writer Andy Haynes headlines DC Improv for a weekend run covering the August 1 weekend.

events.title: CrazySexyCool – The TLC Musical
events.venue: Arena Stage at the Mead Center for American Theater
events.description: A musical celebrating the friendship, story, and legacy of iconic 90s R&B trio TLC, produced at Arena Stage through August 9, 2026.

events.title: Tommy Davidson at DC Improv
events.description: Tommy Davidson, noted as an 'early August' headliner at DC Improv, performs a weekend run covering the August 8 weekend.

events.title: "Dear America" Exhibition — National Gallery of Art
events.venue: National Gallery of Art, 4th St & Constitution Ave NW
events.description: The National Gallery of Art's 'Dear America' exhibition runs through September 20, featuring artwork that explores the American identity from artists across generations. Part of the broader DC250 programming; free admission.

events.title: Lincoln Memorial Undercroft — New Immersive Exhibit
events.venue: Lincoln Memorial, 2 Lincoln Memorial Circle NW
events.description: A century-old secret opens to the public for America's 250th: 15,000 square feet of immersive exhibits beneath the Lincoln Memorial, opening June 25 just in time for the holiday weekend. Visitors can experience the monument's story in a whole new way in this underground space.

events.title: National Air and Space Museum — 50th Anniversary Reopening & New Galleries
events.venue: National Air and Space Museum, 600 Independence Ave SW
events.description: Celebrating its 50th anniversary coinciding with America's 250th, the Smithsonian's National Air and Space Museum reopens five new galleries including exhibits on WWII aircraft, space technologies, and a Robert Rauschenberg flight-themed exploration. Milestone reopening timed for July 2026.

events.title: Fourth at The Wharf – America250 Free Country Music Festival
events.venue: The Wharf – District Pier, Transit Pier, The Grove & M Street Landing
events.description: America250 celebration with free live country music across four outdoor stages (District Pier, Transit Pier Floating Stage, The Grove, M Street Landing). Performers include Melissa Quinn Fox, Chris Ryan Band, Shane Gamble & The Pawnshop Hearts, Shawn Parsons & The Secret Service, and more at 5 pm and 7 pm slots. Event culminates with spectacular views of the NPS fireworks launched from the National Mall at ~9:09 pm. A paid VIP fireworks package ($60+) offers exclusive Dockmaster Building access.

events.title: Fourth at the Wharf — Free Country Concert
events.description: Free outdoor country music concert at The Wharf on the Fourth of July, featuring Megan Quinn Fox, Emily Woodhull, and the Chris Ryan Band. A festive waterfront celebration on Independence Day, with easy views of the Potomac River.

events.title: Last Minute Comedy at DC Comedy Loft
events.description: Last Minute Comedy showcase at the DC Comedy Loft featuring rotating local and touring comedians on the 4th of July weekend.

events.title: National Archives July 4th Celebration (Declaration Reading & Reenactors)
events.description: The National Archives hosts a ceremonial reading of the Declaration of Independence with George Washington, Thomas Jefferson, and Benjamin Franklin reenactors on the steps. Visitors can view the original Declaration inside, enjoy family activities, and sign a facsimile of the Declaration. Extended hours June 22–July 5.

events.title: National Independence Day Parade
events.venue: Constitution Avenue NW (3rd St NW to 12th St SW)
events.description: The official Fourth of July parade of the United States steps off at 3rd Street and Constitution Avenue NW at 10:30 AM, featuring marching bands from all 50 states, fife and drum corps, military units, floats, and giant balloons. Extra-large in 2026 to honor America's semiquincentennial.

events.title: Pearl Street Warehouse – Independence Day Show
events.description: Independence Day live music at Pearl Street Warehouse. Free RSVP show on the holiday Saturday, all ages welcome.

events.title: Salute to America Celebration & Fireworks (America's 250th Birthday)
events.time: 1:00 PM (gates open); evening program 7:00 PM; fireworks ~10:30 PM
events.description: The capstone of America's 250th anniversary brings hundreds of thousands to the Washington Monument grounds for a full day of headline musical performances, military flyovers, first-ever aerobatic demonstrations over DC, and a live-broadcast evening program with the Joint Armed Forces Orchestra — culminating with 850,000 fireworks shells launched from 10 sites including the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool and eight Potomac River barges. Gates/security open at 1 PM. Fireworks begin around 10:30 PM.

events.title: Washington Nationals vs. Pittsburgh Pirates (Independence Day Game)
events.time: Daytime (check MLB schedule for first pitch)
events.venue: Nationals Park, 1500 South Capitol St SE
events.price: Tickets vary; discount code VISITDC for 15% off
events.description: The Washington Nationals play a daytime home game against the Pittsburgh Pirates on Independence Day, a long-standing July 4 tradition designed to wrap up before the evening fireworks festivities across the city. Use code VISITDC when purchasing tickets for 15% off.

events.title: Sunset Cinema – Hamilton (2020)
events.description: Free weekly Sunset Cinema series presented by Pacifico Beer screens Hamilton (2020) on Transit Pier's big LED screen. Seats open at 7 pm; film begins at 7:30 pm. Family-friendly and all ages welcome. Part of the America250 celebration. No tickets needed – just show up.

events.title: Jazz in the Garden: American Sounds — July 10
events.venue: NGA Sculpture Garden, 4th St & Constitution Ave NW
events.description: DC's beloved free Friday-night outdoor concert series returns to the National Gallery of Art Sculpture Garden. The 2026 theme is 'American Sounds,' honoring the nation's 250th: soul, Appalachian folk, salsa, Hill Country blues, New Orleans brass band, and more. Passes distributed via free weekly online lottery (enter the Monday before each show). Limited day-of passes available at the garden entrance from 5 PM.

events.title: Navy Yard BID Friday Night Concert Series ft. Little Bacon Bear
events.venue: Yards Park Boardwalk, 355 Water St SE
events.description: Free Navy Yard Summer Concert Series at the Yards Park boardwalk featuring Little Bacon Bear, a DMV favorite with deep roots in Prince George's County and one of the region's most dynamic voices in music and culture. Family-friendly lyrics, grassy open space, and stunning Anacostia River views.

events.title: Rock the Dock – HariKaraoke Band (Live Band Karaoke Night)
events.description: Free weekly Rock the Dock Friday night concert series presented by Pacifico features the HariKaraoke Band for a special live band karaoke edition. Takes the stage at Transit Pier (970 Wharf Street SW). Bring your voice or just enjoy the show along the Potomac waterfront.

events.title: Family-Friendly C&O Canal Cycling Adventure (San Mar / WABA)
events.venue: C&O Canal Towpath (start location TBD — check waba.org/fun for exact trailhead)
events.description: A family-friendly cycling adventure along the C&O Canal Towpath, hosted by San Mar Family and Community Services and listed on WABA's event calendar. The route is flat, car-free, and well-supported with food and rest stops — riders go at their own pace.

events.title: Outdoor Movie Night: National Treasure at The Wharf
events.venue: Transit Pier, The Wharf, 680 Maine Ave SW
events.description: Free outdoor screening of National Treasure: Book of Secrets (2007) on Transit Pier at The Wharf, presented by Pacifico Beer. A patriotic crowd-pleaser on the waterfront — fitting for the America 250 summer.

events.title: WABA New HQ Opening Celebration Ride
events.venue: WABA New Headquarters, 1110 Hamlin St NE
events.description: Celebrate the grand opening of WABA's brand-new headquarters at 1110 Hamlin St NE in Brookland. Join fellow cyclists for a community meetup and ride marking this milestone for DC's premier bike advocacy organization.

events.title: Shed 5 Flea – 10th Anniversary Special at Eastern Market
events.description: Shed 5 Flea celebrates 10 summers at Eastern Market with one expanded, carefully curated July 12th anniversary show — consolidating the entire season into a single landmark event. Expect a larger-than-usual mix of vintage finds, art, boutique fashion, and handmade goods.

events.title: Sunset Cinema – A League of Their Own (1992)
events.description: Free weekly Sunset Cinema series presented by Pacifico Beer screens A League of Their Own (1992) on Transit Pier. Presented as part of the America250 celebration. Seats available from 7 pm; film at 7:30 pm. All ages welcome, no tickets needed.

events.title: DC Improv 34th Anniversary Show
events.description: The DC Improv celebrates its 34th anniversary (opened summer 1992) with a special one-night event.

events.title: Jazz in the Garden: American Sounds — July 17
events.venue: NGA Sculpture Garden, 4th St & Constitution Ave NW
events.description: DC's beloved free Friday-night outdoor concert series continues at the National Gallery of Art Sculpture Garden. The 2026 theme 'American Sounds' traverses the nation's musical heritage. Passes via free weekly lottery; limited day-of passes at garden entrance at 5 PM.

events.title: Navy Yard BID Friday Night Concert Series – July 17
events.venue: Yards Park Boardwalk, 355 Water St SE
events.description: Free weekly Navy Yard Summer Concert Series at the Yards Park boardwalk. The series runs every Friday June 12 through July 31; specific July 17 performer TBA (full schedule listed as 'coming soon' on official site). Family-friendly, riverside setting with food and beverage options nearby.

events.title: Rock the Dock – Hot Cactus (Top 40)
events.description: Free Friday night Rock the Dock concert series at Transit Pier presents Hot Cactus performing Top 40 hits. Part of The Wharf's weekly summer concert lineup presented by Pacifico. Limited bench seating; food available from Union Pie kiosk and nearby Wharf restaurants.

events.title: Union Stage – Live Music (Jul 18)
events.description: Live music event at Union Stage on Saturday July 18. Artist lineup not yet announced at time of listing; check the official site for updates.

events.title: Union Stage – Live Music (Jul 19)
events.description: Live music event at Union Stage on Sunday July 19. Artist lineup not yet announced at time of listing; check the official site for updates.

events.title: Congress the Band
events.description: Congress the Band performs live at the 9:30 Club on Friday July 24 (opening night of the Jul 25 weekend).

events.title: Union Stage – Live Music (Jul 26)
events.description: Live music event at Union Stage on Sunday July 26. Artist lineup not yet announced at time of listing; check the official site for updates.

events.title: The Washington Roast at DC Improv
events.description: The Washington Roast is a hit comedy show where the city's movers and shakers get roasted. A one-night DC Improv special event.

events.title: Souls of Mischief (w/ Nature Boi & Beau Young Prince)
events.description: Legendary Oakland hip-hop group Souls of Mischief perform at Union Stage on Friday July 31, supported by Nature Boi and Beau Young Prince.

events.title: ALL THE FEELINGS TOUR: Metric, Broken Social Scene & Stars
events.description: Canadian indie-rock triple bill: Metric, Broken Social Scene, and Stars on the All The Feelings Tour at The Anthem on Saturday August 1.

events.title: Lord Huron
events.description: Indie folk-rock band Lord Huron performs at The Anthem on Sunday August 2.

events.title: Houndmouth (w/ Rayland Baxter)
events.description: Indie rock band Houndmouth headlines The Anthem on Friday August 7, supported by Rayland Baxter. Opening night of the Aug 8 weekend.

events.title: KALEO – Way Down We Go Tour (w/ Dogpark)
events.description: Icelandic blues-rock band KALEO plays The Anthem on Saturday August 8 on the Way Down We Go Tour, with Dogpark in support.
